Mar 6, 2006 CONTENTS: ImageMagick Manual pages Pertinent files For more information ImageMagick ----------- ImageMagick, a suite of image manipulation programs, is installed on the Solaris systems. Here is a description of the pa

Solaris systems. Here is a description of the package from its authors: ImageMagick(TM) is a robust collection of tools and libraries to read, write, and manipulate an image in many image formats (over 68 major formats) including pop

formats (over 68 major formats) including popular formats like TIFF, JPEG, PNG, PDF, Photo CD, and GIF. With ImageMagick you can create images dynamically, making it suitable for Web applications. You can also resize, rotate, shar

applications. You can also resize, rotate, sharpen, color reduce, or add special effects to an image and save your completed work in the same or differing image format. Image processing operations are available from the command line

ing operations are available from the command line, as well as through C, C++, and PERL-based programming interfaces. It consists of the following programs: * animate - display a sequence of images on any workstation running X. * combine

images on any workstation running X. * combine - combine images to create new images. * convert - converts an input file using one image format to an output file with the same or differing image format. * display - display an ima

ffering image format. * display - display an image on any workstation running X. * identify - describe the format and characteristics of one or more image files. * import - capture some or all of an X server screen and save the

some or all of an X server screen and save the image to a file. * mogrify - transform an image or sequence of images. * montage - creates a composite image by combining several separate images. Manual pages ------------ Documen

rate images. Manual pages ------------ Documentation is available for the following programs: ImageMagick(1) animate(1) combine(1) convert(1) display(1) identify(1) import(1) mogrify(1) montage(1) miff(

port(1) mogrify(1) montage(1) miff(4) quantize(5) Set your MANPATH environment variable with the command: % setenv MANPATH /usr/sww/pkg/ImageMagick-5.5.4/man:${MANPATH} in order to read them. Pertinent files --------

in order to read them. Pertinent files --------------- Under /usr/sww/pkg/ImageMagick-5.5.4: Programs: bin/Magick-config bin/animate bin/combine bin/convert bin/display bin/identify bin/import bin/mogrify bin/montage Config

bin/import bin/mogrify bin/montage Config file: share/ImageMagick/delegates.mgk Include files: under include/magick Manual pages: under man Libraries: lib/libMagick.so lib/libMagick.so.5 lib/libMagick.la lib/libMagick.a lib/libMa

5 lib/libMagick.la lib/libMagick.a lib/libMagick.so.5.0.11 For more information -------------------- Please see the "ImageMagick - Image Conversion and Manipulation Software" website at . For questions/problems with the installation, ple
